subtopics.title: SELECT
subtopics.explanation: The SELECT statement is the fundamental SQL command for querying data from a database. It specifies which columns to retrieve and can include filtering, sorting, and aggregation.
subtopics.example: Retrieve customer names and emails from the customers table.
subtopics.code: SELECT name, email FROM customers WHERE country = 'USA' ORDER BY name;

subtopics.title: JOIN
subtopics.explanation: JOIN combines rows from two or more tables based on a related column. Inner JOIN returns only matching rows, while LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table with matches from the right.
subtopics.example: Combine orders with customer information to see who placed each order.
subtopics.code: SELECT orders.id, customers.name FROM orders INNER JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.id;

subtopics.title: GROUP BY
subtopics.explanation: GROUP BY groups rows that have the same values in specified columns, allowing aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG) to be applied to each group. Essential for summary reports.
subtopics.example: Calculate total sales per product category.
subtopics.code: SELECT category, SUM(sales) AS total_sales FROM products GROUP BY category ORDER BY total_sales DESC;
